what_it_does: An acceptance script that exercises the runtime settable MODE_AUTODETECT slot end-to-end: it creates a temporary store and Suite, verifies the default 'off' behavior, sets MODE_AUTODETECT to 'suggest'/'auto'/'off' through set_rhm_config, checks that the live Suite attribute and capabilities reflect the new value, validates autodetect_mode actions under each setting, rejects invalid values fail-loud, confirms other config slots still round-trip, and reloads a fresh Suite to confirm persistence.
observations: Uses a temporary FsStore and removes it in a try/finally block.
Pops COMPANY_MODE_AUTODETECT from the environment to avoid shadowing the default floor.
Defines a global PASS counter incremented by an inline check() helper.
Calls set_rhm_config with the uppercase key 'MODE_AUTODETECT' but asserts persistence under the lowercase slot 'mode_autodetect'.
Inspects the private method suite._rhm_cfg() to verify persistence.
Tests autodetect_mode return values: 'noop' for off, 'suggested' for suggest, 'switched' for auto.
Expects ValueError when setting 'bananas' and when autodetecting 'not-a-mode'.
Creates a second Suite on the same store to test reload-survival of persisted config.
standouts: End-to-end acceptance of making MODE_AUTODETECT runtime-settable via the existing config path.
Reload-survival check with a fresh Suite reading the persisted value.
Fail-loud validation checks for both config writes and fabricated candidate modes.
Uppercase user-facing key mapped to a lowercase node-config slot.
conventions: Inline assertion helper with global pass counter rather than a pytest-style test.
Docstring-first file with design rationale and ticket references.
Manual sys.path insertion and env cleanup at the top of the script.
Try/finally cleanup of the temporary store directory.
concerns: Asserts on the private method _rhm_cfg(), coupling tests to internal representation.
Uses a mutable global PASS counter.
Directly manipulates os.environ within the test.
Asserts the internal lowercase slot name 'mode_autodetect' as a persistence contract.
The file is named as an acceptance test but runs as a plain script without a main guard or pytest harness.
notes: A runtime-config acceptance script that treats MODE_AUTODETECT as a first-class settable config slot and verifies it persists, re-resolves, and survives reload.
questions: What do GC6, E2-live, and X17 re-resolve refer to in this codebase?
What nodes are discovered from the nodes directory and what modes do they expose?
Where is set_rhm_config implemented and how does it map uppercase keys to lowercase slots?
Is this script intended to be run by pytest or directly as a Python file?
What is the role of fabric.config in this test?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose in the docstring is to prove MODE_AUTODETECT is runtime-settable, persistent, re-resolved, and fail-loud; the file executes exactly that sequence of checks.
apparent_themes: Backward compatibility with existing config slots
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish and enforce that MODE_AUTODETECT behaves like the existing model/persona/voice slots: settable through the standard config path, immediately honored, persisted to the store, and reloadable without regression.
novelty: It tests the live re-resolution of what was previously a read-only env-resolved class constant and the uppercase-to-lowercase config key mapping for a new slot.
contribution: A concrete, runnable acceptance scenario that documents the expected behavior of MODE_AUTODETECT as a runtime-settable, persistent config slot and guards against regressions in autodetect_mode and related config round-trips.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance script that verifies MODE_AUTODETECT can be set at runtime via set_rhm_config, persists under the lowercase slot mode_autodetect, re-resolves on the live Suite, drives autodetect_mode behavior, rejects invalid values, and survives a Suite reload.
intention_for_embedding: To prove the GC6/E2-live change that makes MODE_AUTODETECT a runtime-settable, persistent config slot while preserving existing config slot behavior and fail-loud validation.

symbols.does: Increments a global PASS counter if cond is true, otherwise raises AssertionError with the given label.
symbols.description: An inline assertion helper used throughout the acceptance script; it prints an 'ok' message on success and maintains the PASS count that is reported in the final summary line.
